auto_approve.mechanism: kiro_mcp_auto_approve: Kiro supports per-server or per-tool auto-approval configuration to suppress confirmation prompts

enterprise_management.mechanism: Kiro does not document organization-level MCP management

env_var_expansion.mechanism: kiro_mcp_env_var_expansion: Environment variables referenced dynamically using ${VARIABLE_NAME} syntax within MCP config values; recommended pattern for secrets instead of hardcoded values

marketplace.mechanism: Kiro does not have an in-IDE MCP marketplace

oauth_support.mechanism: Kiro MCP does not document OAuth 2.0 authentication; remote server auth uses static headers

resource_referencing.mechanism: Kiro does not document MCP resource referencing

tool_filtering.mechanism: kiro_mcp_disabled_tools: Kiro supports per-server tool disable configuration to control which MCP tools are available to the agent

transport_types.mechanism: Kiro supports stdio (local process via command + args) and remote (HTTPS endpoint or localhost HTTP via url + optional headers) server types

providerExtensions.name: autoApprove per-server tool list (or wildcard)
providerExtensions.summary: Per-server 'autoApprove' field accepting a tool-name array or '*' wildcard to skip user confirmation.

providerExtensions.name: disabledTools per-server list
providerExtensions.summary: Per-server 'disabledTools' array suppressing specific tools without disabling the entire server.

providerExtensions.name: disabled flag per server entry
providerExtensions.summary: Per-server 'disabled: true' flag deactivating a server without removing its configuration. Allows temporarily disabling a server while preserving its definition.

providerExtensions.name: Remote server support (url + headers)
providerExtensions.summary: Remote MCP servers configured with 'url' (HTTPS or localhost HTTP endpoint) and optional 'headers' map for authentication. Configuration applies automatically upon saving.

providerExtensions.name: Environment variable expansion in MCP config values
providerExtensions.summary: MCP config values support ${VARIABLE_NAME} syntax to reference environment variables at runtime. Recommended for API keys and secrets to avoid storing sensitive values directly in config files.
